parameters.name: TRAIN_TARGET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which segmentation target to train."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Choose which segmentation target to train. BOTH runs nucleus and whole-cell training sequentially with separate target-specific model IDs and evidence.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by TRAIN_TARGET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "NUCLEUS".

parameters.name: TRAINING_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Select the locked epoch/checkpoint preset."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Select the locked epoch/checkpoint preset. TEST is for diagnostic pipeline checks only; FAST through ULTRA produce promoted models when a promoted model ID is supplied.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by TRAINING_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "TEST".

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact image channel names used to train the nucleus target. Change only to match project channel names or an explicitly documented training design.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact image channel names used to train the whole-cell target, in Cellpose channel order. Keep this auditable because channel order changes the trained model.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["AF555", "AF488", "DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: NUC_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted nucleus mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest nucleus mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny specks or fragmented nuclear debris. Decrease it only if true nuclei are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream classification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: CELL_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted cell mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest whole-cell mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny debris or fragmented cell masks. Decrease it only if true cells are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream quantification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Batch compatible Cellpose work across images or regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Enables ASTRA's batch-oriented Cellpose training runtime behavior. Leave enabled for normal training unless diagnosing runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use image calibration for physical-scale Cellpose input."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Uses image calibration when staging training inputs for the configured runtime. Disable only for controlled diagnostics because it changes how training images are scaled.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_GUI_NOTIFICATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Show QuPath notifications for fatal errors, warnings, and successful completion."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Show QuPath notifications for fatal errors, warnings, and successful completion. Disable only for scripted batch diagnostics.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Choose which project images contribute training data. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image; PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION, match names against original imported image names rather than QuPath display names.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: MODE_LADDER
parameters.defaultGroovy:
[
    TEST    : [epochs: 1, save_every: 1],
    FAST    : [epochs: 50, save_every: 10],
    BALANCED: [epochs: 100, save_every: 5],
    FULL    : [epochs: 200, save_every: 2],
    ULTRA   : [epochs: 500, save_every: 1],
]
parameters.helpGroovy: "Locked training preset table."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Locked training preset table. Edit only for an intentional protocol change because epoch count and checkpoint cadence affect model auditability.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: [
    TEST    : [epochs: 1, save_every: 1],
    FAST    : [epochs: 50, save_every: 10],
    BALANCED: [epochs: 100, save_every: 5],
    FULL    : [epochs: 200, save_every: 2],
    ULTRA   : [epochs: 500, save_every: 1],
].

parameters.name: LEARNING_RATE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose training learning rate shared across presets."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose training learning rate shared across presets. Changing it creates a different training protocol and should be recorded in run provenance.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1.0e-5.

parameters.name: WEIGHT_DECAY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose weight-decay value."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose weight-decay value. Change only for a deliberate training protocol adjustment.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 0.10.

parameters.name: BATCH_SIZE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose training batch size."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose training batch size. Increase only when the runtime environment has been validated for the larger memory load.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1.

parameters.name: MIN_TRAIN_MASKS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum mask count required by the training export/launch path."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum mask count required by the training export/launch path. Raising it tightens data sufficiency checks before Cellpose runs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 1.

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class name for nucleus ground-truth annotations. A mismatch fails training-region validation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: "Nucleus".

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class name for whole-cell ground-truth annotations. A mismatch fails training-region validation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Cell".

parameters.name: REGION_CLASS_TRAINING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for region class training."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class name for training regions. Only ground truth inside these regions contributes to training.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Training".

parameters.name: REGION_CLASS_VALIDATION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for region class validation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class name for validation regions. Non-TEST training requires usable validation regions for best-epoch selection.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Validation".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves nucleus model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus training initializer selector. MODEL_NAME uses a named Cellpose initializer, SAVED uses the promoted nucleus model, and FILE uses an explicit model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model name used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus model file path used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. ASTRA never chooses a saved model automatically at runtime.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves cell model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell training initializer selector. MODEL_NAME uses a named Cellpose initializer, SAVED uses the promoted cell model, and FILE uses an explicit model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by CELL_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell Cellpose model name used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute whole-cell model file path used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. ASTRA never chooses a saved model automatically at runtime.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELLPOSE_FORK_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Required marker checked during runtime preflight to confirm the configured Cellpose installation appears to be the ASTRA fork."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Required marker checked during runtime preflight to confirm the configured Cellpose installation appears to be the ASTRA fork.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: "astra".

parameters.name: PRINT_VERDICT_BLOCK
parameters.helpGroovy: "Print the final evidence/verdict recap."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Print the final evidence/verdict recap. Leave enabled for reviewable training logs.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: EXPORT_LOSS_CURVES_CSV
parameters.helpGroovy: "Control whether ASTRA writes export loss curves csv."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Retired. Leave false. Training loss curves are written only as promoted-model evidence after non-TEST promotion.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: EXPORT_SUMMARY_JSON
parameters.helpGroovy: "Control whether ASTRA writes export summary json."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Retired. Leave false. Training summaries are written only as promoted-model evidence after non-TEST promotion.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: OVERLAP_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Pixel overlap for Cellpose tiling during training/runtime staging."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Pixel overlap for Cellpose tiling during training/runtime staging. Change only for a documented tiling protocol adjustment.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 60.

parameters.name: NTHREADS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Worker thread count requested from the Cellpose runtime."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Worker thread count requested from the Cellpose runtime. Lower this for constrained machines or shared workstations.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: Math.max(1, Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors() - 1).

parameters.name: READ_RESULTS_ASYNC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Read Cellpose results asynchronously through the extension runtime."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Read Cellpose results asynchronously through the extension runtime. Disable only while diagnosing runtime synchronization issues.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: NUC_PROMOTED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit promoted nucleus model ID written under astra/models/nucleus/<model_id>."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit promoted nucleus model ID written under astra/models/nucleus/<model_id>. Required outside TEST mode for nucleus training.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_PROMOTED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit promoted whole-cell model ID written under astra/models/cell/<model_id>."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit promoted whole-cell model ID written under astra/models/cell/<model_id>. Required outside TEST mode for cell training.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: DEBUG
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Print additional training control-flow diagnostics.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_IO
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug io diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Print additional filesystem and artifact path diagnostics.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_SCORE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug score diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Print additional training score/evidence diagnostics.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_CP
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug cp diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Print additional Cellpose command/runtime diagnostics.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: TUNE_TARGET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Selects which biological target is tuned."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Selects which biological target is tuned. NUCLEUS and CELL tune one target-specific model/parameter contract; BOTH runs the same audited workflow separately for each target.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by TUNE_TARGET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "NUCLEUS".

parameters.name: SEARCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls tuning intensity."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls tuning intensity. TEST exercises runability only and writes no durable tuning CSV, QC figure, or best_params.json; FAST/BALANCED/FULL/ULTRA perform increasingly broad deterministic searches.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by SEARCH_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "TEST".

parameters.name: SEARCH_STYLE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls whether selected tiers are optimized together or one stage at a time."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls whether selected tiers are optimized together or one stage at a time. STAGED is easier to audit; JOINT explores interactions between knobs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by SEARCH_STYLE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "STAGED".

parameters.name: TIERS_TO_RUN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Ordered tuning tiers to evaluate."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Ordered tuning tiers to evaluate. Tier choices define which Cellpose/SAM knobs can change and are recorded in tuning outputs for reproducibility.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: [1].

parameters.name: SCORE_METRIC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Primary objective used to rank candidate parameters."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Primary objective used to rank candidate parameters. DICE_PQ penalizes false positives/negatives, DICE is legacy overlap-only behavior, and IOU_PQ uses IoU-based matching.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by SCORE_METRIC_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "DICE_PQ".

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Ordered image channels passed to Cellpose for nucleus tuning. Names must exactly match the QuPath image channel labels.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Ordered image channels passed to Cellpose for whole-cell tuning. Include only channels intended to inform the cell detector.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["AF555", "AF488", "DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Batch compatible Cellpose work across images or regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Runs each tuning parameter set through ASTRA's batch-oriented Cellpose path across eligible images. Leave enabled for normal tuning unless diagnosing runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use image calibration for physical-scale Cellpose input."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Uses image calibration when staging tuning inputs for the configured runtime. Disable only for controlled diagnostics because it changes detector input scale.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_GUI_NOTIFICATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Shows QuPath notifications for fatal tuning errors, replacement decisions, and completion."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Shows QuPath notifications for fatal tuning errors, replacement decisions, and completion. Console logs remain the detailed audit trail.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Selects which project images contribute validation regions to tuning. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image; PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION, matches against original imported names instead of QuPath display names.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: QuPath annotation class containing nucleus ground truth. The class name must match exactly or tuning fails before search.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: "Nucleus".

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: QuPath annotation class containing whole-cell ground truth. The class name must match exactly or tuning fails before search.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Cell".

parameters.name: REGION_CLASS_VALIDATION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for region class validation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: QuPath annotation class marking validation regions used for tuning. Each region must contain target-specific ground truth.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Validation".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves nucleus model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus model selector. MODEL_NAME uses a Cellpose model name, SAVED uses the promoted nucleus model, and FILE uses an explicit model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model name used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

Default value: "NUCLEUS".

parameters.name: VALIDATION_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls validation intensity."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls validation intensity. TEST exercises runability only and writes no durable validation CSV, summary JSON, or QC output; FAST/BALANCED/FULL/ULTRA evaluate increasingly broad deterministic validation sets.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by VALIDATION_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "FULL".

parameters.name: SCORE_METRIC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Primary validation metric."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Primary validation metric. DICE_PQ and IOU_PQ penalize false positives/false negatives through panoptic-quality scoring; DICE reports mean best overlap without an explicit false-positive penalty.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by SCORE_METRIC_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "DICE_PQ".

parameters.name: SEGMENTATION_PRESET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the Cellpose-SAM inference preset."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: GUI-friendly Cellpose-SAM validation preset used for defaulted inference knobs. Tuned best-params values remain authoritative when present.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Allowed values are controlled by SEGMENTATION_PRESET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SEGMENTATION_PRESET_BALANCED.

Example: use BALANCED for routine runs, STRICT for fewer weak detections, SENSITIVE for weak-object recovery, and CUSTOM only after deliberate tuning.

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channels passed to Cellpose for nucleus validation. Names must match the project image channels exactly and should include only segmentation-relevant channels.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNELS_FOR_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channels for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channels passed to Cellpose for whole-cell validation. Names must match the project image channels exactly and should include only cell-boundary or morphology-relevant channels.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["AF555", "AF488", "DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Batch compatible Cellpose work across images or regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Runs validation detections through ASTRA's batch-oriented Cellpose path for the selected parameter set. Leave enabled for normal validation unless diagnosing runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use image calibration for physical-scale Cellpose input."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Uses image calibration when staging validation inputs for the configured runtime. Disable only for controlled diagnostics because it changes detector input scale.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_GUI_NOTIFICATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Shows QuPath notifications for validation failure and completion."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Shows QuPath notifications for validation failure and completion. Disabling this affects only GUI messaging; fatal errors still throw and appear in the run log.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which project images contribute validation regions. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image; PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION, matches SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES against original imported image names instead of QuPath display names. Use only when project entries were renamed.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_NUCLEUS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class for nucleus ground-truth annotations. These objects define the reference masks for nucleus validation scoring.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: "Nucleus".

parameters.name: GT_CLASS_CELL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for gt class cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class for whole-cell ground-truth annotations. These objects define the reference masks for cell validation scoring.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Cell".

parameters.name: REGION_CLASS_VALIDATION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for region class validation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class for validation-region annotations. Validation only scores ground truth and predictions inside these regions.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Validation".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves nucleus model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus model selector. MODEL_NAME uses a Cellpose model name, SAVED uses the promoted nucleus model, and FILE uses an explicit model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model name used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus model file path used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. Runtime selection is deterministic and never picks newest, best, or only automatically.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves cell model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell model selector. MODEL_NAME uses a Cellpose model name, SAVED uses the promoted cell model, and FILE uses an explicit model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by CELL_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell Cellpose model name used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute whole-cell model file path used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. Runtime selection is deterministic and never picks newest, best, or only automatically.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_PARAM_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Nucleus validation parameter selector."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus validation parameter selector. SAVED uses model-scoped tuning output for the selected nucleus model; BEST_PARAMS_FILE reads the explicit nucleus tuning JSON path.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_PARAM_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "SAVED".

parameters.name: NUC_BEST_PARAMS_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Absolute nucleus best-parameters JSON path used when NUC_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus best-parameters JSON path used when NUC_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_PARAM_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Whole-cell validation parameter selector."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell validation parameter selector. SAVED uses model-scoped tuning output for the selected cell model; BEST_PARAMS_FILE reads the explicit cell tuning JSON path.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by CELL_PARAM_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "SAVED".

parameters.name: CELL_BEST_PARAMS_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Absolute whole-cell best-parameters JSON path used when CELL_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute whole-cell best-parameters JSON path used when CELL_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELLPOSE_FORK_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Text marker expected from the configured Cellpose runtime to confirm the ASTRA fork."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Text marker expected from the configured Cellpose runtime to confirm the ASTRA fork. Leave as astra unless validating a deliberately different audited runtime.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: "astra".

parameters.name: ALLOW_PER_TARGET_OVERRIDES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enables target-specific scoring overrides."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Enables target-specific scoring overrides. Leave false to keep nucleus and cell validation on the same metric, threshold, assignment, and FP/FN weighting contract.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: ASSIGN_METHOD_DEFAULT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Default matching strategy for PQ-style validation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Default matching strategy for PQ-style validation. AUTO chooses deterministic optimal assignment for small problems and greedy assignment above AUTO_OPT_MAX.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by ASSIGN_METHOD_DEFAULT_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "AUTO".

parameters.name: AUTO_OPT_MAX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum assignment matrix dimension where AUTO uses optimal Hungarian matching."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum assignment matrix dimension where AUTO uses optimal Hungarian matching. Larger sets use greedy matching to keep validation runtime bounded.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 50.

parameters.name: MATCH_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for match threshold default."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum Dice/IoU overlap required for a prediction-ground-truth match in PQ-style validation scoring.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.50d.

parameters.name: FP_WEIGHT_DEFAULT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Default false-positive weight in recognition-quality scoring."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Default false-positive weight in recognition-quality scoring. Higher values penalize extra predicted objects more strongly.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 0.50d.

parameters.name: FN_WEIGHT_DEFAULT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Default false-negative weight in recognition-quality scoring."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Default false-negative weight in recognition-quality scoring. Higher values penalize missed ground-truth objects more strongly.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 0.50d.

[NUCLEUS: [diameter_um: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO,
                                                cellprob   : CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD,

                                                niter      : CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO,
                                                min_mask_equivalent_diameter_um: 1.0d,
                                                norm_pmin  : CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN,

parameters.helpGroovy: "Pinned per-target defaults used when best_params.json omits an untuned Cellpose knob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Pinned per-target defaults used when best_params.json omits an untuned Cellpose knob. Diameter 0.0 and niter 0 omit explicit builder values, while min_mask_equivalent_diameter_um is converted to Cellpose --min_size from each image calibration.

Default value: [NUCLEUS: [diameter_um: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO,

                                                min_mask_equivalent_diameter_um: 1.0d,
                                                norm_pmin  : CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN,
                                                norm_pmax  : CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMAX,
                                                simplify_px: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX],
                                      CELL   : [diameter_um: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO,
                                                cellprob   : CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD,
                                                flow       : CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD,
                                                niter      : CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO,
                                                min_mask_equivalent_diameter_um: 1.0d,
                                                norm_pmin  : CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN,
                                                norm_pmax  : CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMAX,
                                                simplify_px: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX]].

parameters.name: INJECT_DEFAULTS_FOR_UNTUNED_KNOBS
parameters.helpGroovy: "When true, explicitly applies the pinned upstream Cellpose defaults above for knobs omitted by tuning output."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When true, explicitly applies the pinned upstream Cellpose defaults above for knobs omitted by tuning output. Disable only when intentionally testing raw runtime-default behavior.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: PRINT_PARAM_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls validation parameter reporting."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls validation parameter reporting. ALL_TAGGED prints tuned and defaulted knobs; ONLY_TUNED prints tuned knobs plus a compact default policy summary.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by PRINT_PARAM_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "ALL_TAGGED".

parameters.name: DEBUG
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Enables extra validation diagnostics in the run log. Use for development or audit triage, not as a substitute for exported validation artifacts.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_IO
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug io diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Logs additional filesystem and image-staging diagnostics for validation runs.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_SCORE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug score diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Logs additional matching and scoring diagnostics for validation regions.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DEBUG_CP
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable debug cp diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Logs additional Cellpose runtime details for validation inference.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: USE_GPU
parameters.helpGroovy: "Run Cellpose on GPU when the configured runtime supports it."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Requests GPU acceleration for Cellpose validation runs. Disable only when the configured runtime/GPU is unavailable; this affects execution performance, not scoring rules.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: CONSTRAIN_TO_PARENT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Configure constrain to parent for Validation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Constrains prediction ROIs to their parent validation region before scoring, preventing detections outside the audited region from affecting metrics.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: CLEAR_DETECTIONS_BEFORE_EVAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Retains ASTRA's internal pre-evaluation cleanup switch."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Retains ASTRA's internal pre-evaluation cleanup switch. Validation refuses to run when pre-existing detections are present; remove or archive unrelated detections before validation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: CLEAR_DETECTIONS_AFTER_EVAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Clears temporary ASTRA validation detections after scoring."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Clears temporary ASTRA validation detections after scoring. This does not permit clearing unrelated pre-existing detections, which block validation before inference.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: VALIDATION_SUBSET_CAP_TEST
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum validation regions used in TEST mode; TEST is for runability only and writes no durable validation artifacts."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum validation regions used in TEST mode; TEST is for runability only and writes no durable validation artifacts.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 1.

parameters.name: VALIDATION_SUBSET_CAP_FAST
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum validation regions used in FAST mode."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum validation regions used in FAST mode. FAST remains deterministic and is intended for quick iteration rather than final reporting.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 4.

parameters.name: VALIDATION_SUBSET_CAP_BALANCED
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum validation regions used in BALANCED mode; zero means all eligible regions are evaluated."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum validation regions used in BALANCED mode; zero means all eligible regions are evaluated.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: 0.

parameters.name: MODES_TO_RUN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which pipeline stages run, in order."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Vascular stages to run in order. AUTO_BUILD_CLASSIFIERS and AUTO_SELECT_ROIS are not-yet-live-validated helpers; a defensible baseline remains manual classifier review. Treat generated classifiers as candidates requiring biological review. Routine publication runs normally use GENERATE_REGIONS, DETECT_CELLS, then QUANTIFY. Export and reset are separate validated script actions.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by MODES_TO_RUN_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: ["GENERATE_REGIONS", "DETECT_CELLS", "QUANTIFY"].

parameters.name: SCRIPT_ACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Internal launcher action. RUN executes selected stages; EXPORT, RESET_IMAGE, and RESET_PROJECT are set only by header buttons and are not editable run stages."

parameters.name: CLASSIFIER_LUMEN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for classifier lumen."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Name of the reviewed QuPath pixel classifier used to identify lumen/background space during GENERATE_REGIONS.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "lumen".

parameters.name: CLASSIFIER_SMOOTH_MUSCLE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for classifier smooth muscle."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Name of the reviewed QuPath pixel classifier used to define the smooth-muscle wall region for vascular quantification.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "smooth_muscle".

parameters.name: CLASSIFIER_ENDOTHELIUM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for classifier endothelium."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Name of the reviewed QuPath pixel classifier used to define the endothelial region for vascular quantification.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: "endothelium".

parameters.name: CLASS_ROI
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the parent vessel ROI class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class for manually reviewed vessel parent regions. Vascular stages mutate only within these ROI annotations.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "ROI".

parameters.name: CLASS_TRACE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the manually drawn lumen trace class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class for manually drawn lumen trace annotations inside each vessel ROI.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Trace".

parameters.name: CLASS_LUMEN_OUTPUT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for class lumen output."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: QuPath class assigned to ASTRA-generated lumen annotations during region generation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

parameters.name: CHANNEL_DAPI

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "DAPI".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNEL_WGA
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channel wga."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channel used as the membrane/boundary signal for whole-cell segmentation and vascular topology support.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "AF488".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNEL_ASMA
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channel asma."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channel used as the smooth-muscle marker for wall classification and VSMC calls.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "AF555".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CHANNEL_CD31
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by channel cd31."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
