parameters.name: CELLPOSE_CELL_CHANNELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by cellpose cell channels."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Channels passed to whole-cell detection. These are segmentation channels, not all available image channels; include only channels intentionally used for cell segmentation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: [CHANNEL_ASMA, CHANNEL_WGA, CHANNEL_DAPI].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: LUMEN_MIN_AREA_CAL_INIT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Initial minimum lumen area in square microns for classifier-derived lumen search."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Initial minimum lumen area in square microns for classifier-derived lumen search. Raising this rejects smaller lumen candidates.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 50.0d.

parameters.name: LUMEN_MIN_HOLE_AREA_CAL_INIT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Initial minimum hole area in square microns used while resolving lumen topology from classifier output."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Initial minimum hole area in square microns used while resolving lumen topology from classifier output.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 50.0d.

parameters.name: LUMEN_MAX_ATTEMPTS_EXPAND
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum expansion attempts when the lumen search must grow from the manual trace to find a valid lumen candidate."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum expansion attempts when the lumen search must grow from the manual trace to find a valid lumen candidate.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 8.

parameters.name: LUMEN_MAX_ATTEMPTS_BISECT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Maximum bisection attempts used to refine lumen search boundaries after expansion."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Maximum bisection attempts used to refine lumen search boundaries after expansion.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 10.

parameters.name: LUMEN_AREA_LOWER_BOUND_CAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Lower calibrated lumen-area bound used during lumen convergence; keep at zero unless intentionally excluding tiny lumen candidates."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Lower calibrated lumen-area bound used during lumen convergence; keep at zero unless intentionally excluding tiny lumen candidates.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 0.0d.

parameters.name: LUMEN_AREA_EPS_CAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Calibrated lumen-area tolerance used when convergence compares candidate lumen sizes."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Calibrated lumen-area tolerance used when convergence compares candidate lumen sizes.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 0.5d.

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves nucleus model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus model selector. MODEL_NAME uses a named Cellpose initializer, SAVED uses the promoted ASTRA nucleus model, and FILE uses an explicit nucleus model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model name used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus model file used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved nucleus model ID used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. ASTRA resolves this under projectRoot/astra/models/nucleus/<model_id>/ and records model metadata plus SHA-256.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves cell model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell model selector. MODEL_NAME uses a named Cellpose initializer, SAVED uses the promoted ASTRA cell model, and FILE uses an explicit cell model file.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by CELL_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MODEL_NAME".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for cell."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell Cellpose model name used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "cpsam".

parameters.name: CELL_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute whole-cell model file used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Explicit saved cell model ID used when CELL_MODEL_SOURCE is SAVED. ASTRA resolves this under projectRoot/astra/models/cell/<model_id>/ and records model metadata plus SHA-256.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_PARAM_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Nucleus parameter selector."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus parameter selector. MANUAL uses explicit nucleus parameters; SAVED reads model-scoped tuning params for the selected nucleus model; BEST_PARAMS_FILE reads an explicit audited JSON path.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_PARAM_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MANUAL".

parameters.name: NUC_BEST_PARAMS_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Absolute nucleus best-parameters file used when NUC_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus best-parameters file used when NUC_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: CELL_PARAM_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Whole-cell parameter selector."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell parameter selector. MANUAL uses explicit whole-cell parameters; SAVED reads model-scoped tuning params for the selected cell model; BEST_PARAMS_FILE reads an explicit audited JSON path.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by CELL_PARAM_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MANUAL".

parameters.name: CELL_BEST_PARAMS_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Absolute whole-cell best-parameters file used when CELL_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute whole-cell best-parameters file used when CELL_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: USE_GPU
parameters.helpGroovy: "Run Cellpose on GPU when the configured runtime supports it."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Requests GPU acceleration for Cellpose execution. Disable only when the configured runtime/GPU is unavailable; this affects execution performance, not vascular scoring rules.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: TILE_SIZE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose tile size in pixels for vascular detection staging."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose tile size in pixels for vascular detection staging. Larger tiles reduce tiling overhead but increase runtime memory demand.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1024.

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Batch compatible Cellpose work across images or regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Enables ASTRA's batch-oriented Cellpose runtime behavior for project-scale vascular runs. Leave enabled for normal analysis unless diagnosing runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use image calibration for physical-scale Cellpose input."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Uses image calibration to convert positive micron diameter values into pixel-space Cellpose inputs. Diameter 0.0 still preserves Cellpose auto-diameter behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_GUI_NOTIFICATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Shows QuPath notifications for vascular failure and completion."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Shows QuPath notifications for vascular failure and completion. Disabling this affects only GUI messaging; fatal errors still throw and appear in the run log.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_VIEWER_PROGRESS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Focuses the QuPath viewer on the current vascular image/region during long runs."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Focuses the QuPath viewer on the current vascular image/region during long runs. This is display-only and does not affect measurements.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: FOCUS_VIEWER_ON_IMAGE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Moves the viewer to each image as vascular processing begins when viewer progress is enabled."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Moves the viewer to each image as vascular processing begins when viewer progress is enabled.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: FOCUS_VIEWER_ON_ROI
parameters.helpGroovy: "Moves the viewer to each vessel ROI as vascular processing advances when viewer progress is enabled."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Moves the viewer to each vessel ROI as vascular processing advances when viewer progress is enabled.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: VIEWER_FOCUS_PADDING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Viewer-only padding multiplier around focused vessel regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Viewer-only padding multiplier around focused vessel regions.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: 1.35d.

parameters.name: VIEWER_FOCUS_MIN_VIEW_SIZE_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum viewer field size in pixels when focusing small vessel regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum viewer field size in pixels when focusing small vessel regions.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: 256.0d.

parameters.name: LOG_VIEWER_PROGRESS_FAILURES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable log viewer progress failures diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Logs viewer-focus failures for GUI debugging. Viewer-focus failures are display-only and never change vascular results.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which project images or vessel ROIs are analyzed. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image, SELECTED_ANALYSIS_REGION uses the currently selected annotation whose class matches CLASS_ROI, and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION, matches SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES against original imported image names instead of QuPath display names. Use only when project entries were renamed.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: RESULTS_FOLDER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the project-relative ASTRA results folder."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Analysis results subfolder under projectRoot/astra/results. Vascular defaults to astra/results/vascular.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "vascular".

parameters.name: RESULTS_BASENAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the exported result filename prefix."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional CSV basename. Leave blank to let ASTRA derive a deterministic name from the image or project context.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: EXPORT_DIAGNOSTIC_COLUMNS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Control whether ASTRA writes export diagnostic columns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Adds ASTRA-internal per-cell audit columns to vascular exports. Leave disabled for publication-facing CSVs unless debugging segmentation or multinucleated-cell handling.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: USE_NUCLEI
parameters.helpGroovy: "Runs nucleus detection and assigns nucleus ROIs to vascular cell objects."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Runs nucleus detection and assigns nucleus ROIs to vascular cell objects. Disable only for a deliberate no-nucleus diagnostic workflow.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SEGMENTATION_PRESET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the Cellpose-SAM inference preset."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: GUI-friendly Cellpose-SAM inference preset. BALANCED matches ASTRA's previous defaults, STRICT reduces weak detections, SENSITIVE recovers weaker objects, and CUSTOM uses the explicit advanced detector knobs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Allowed values are controlled by SEGMENTATION_PRESET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SEGMENTATION_PRESET_BALANCED.

Example: use BALANCED for routine runs, STRICT for fewer weak detections, SENSITIVE for weak-object recovery, and CUSTOM only after deliberate tuning.

parameters.name: NUC_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by nucleus diameter um."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus diameter in microns for Cellpose. The default 0.0 leaves diameter unspecified, matching upstream Cellpose auto-diameter behavior; set a positive value only after validating nucleus mask scale.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_CELLPROB
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose cellprob acceptance for nucleus cellprob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus cellprob threshold. The default 0.0 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; increasing it makes detection stricter and can remove weak nuclei before vascular measurements.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_FLOW
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose flow acceptance for nucleus flow."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus flow threshold. The default 0.4 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; changing it alters which nucleus masks are accepted.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_NITER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose iteration count for nucleus niter."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus iteration count. The default 0 omits the explicit niter parameter so Cellpose uses its automatic/proportional runtime behavior; positive values force a fixed iteration count.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted nucleus mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest nucleus mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny specks or fragmented nuclear debris. Decrease it only if true nuclei are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream classification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: CELL_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by cell diameter um."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell diameter in microns for Cellpose. The default 0.0 leaves diameter unspecified, matching upstream Cellpose auto-diameter behavior; set a positive value only after validating cell mask scale.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO.

parameters.name: CELL_CELLPROB
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose cellprob acceptance for cell cellprob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell cellprob threshold. The default 0.0 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; increasing it makes detection stricter and can remove weak cell masks before vascular quantification.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: CELL_FLOW
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose flow acceptance for cell flow."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell flow threshold. The default 0.4 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; changing it alters which cell masks are accepted.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: CELL_NITER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose iteration count for cell niter."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell iteration count. The default 0 omits the explicit niter parameter so Cellpose uses its automatic/proportional runtime behavior; positive values force a fixed iteration count.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO.

parameters.name: CELL_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted cell mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest whole-cell mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny debris or fragmented cell masks. Decrease it only if true cells are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream quantification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection. The default 0.0 disables simplification; increasing it can change mask geometry, hierarchy membership, and measurements.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMIN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. The ASTRA default is 1.0; change only after image-intensity review because it affects detector input scaling and segmentation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMAX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. The ASTRA default is 99.0; change only after image-intensity review because it affects detector input scaling and segmentation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMAX.

parameters.name: SAM_NTHREADS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose-SAM thread count."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose-SAM thread count. Zero leaves runtime threading automatic; changing it affects performance, not vascular scoring logic.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: 0.

parameters.name: SAM_READ_RESULTS_ASYNC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running. Leave disabled unless runtime profiling shows a benefit; it affects execution behavior, not vascular scoring rules.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: MULTINUCLEATED_CELL_POLICY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls how candidate cells containing multiple assigned nuclei are handled."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls how candidate cells containing multiple assigned nuclei are handled. EXCLUDE omits them from ordinary vascular quantification while recording rare-event counts; INCLUDE_AS_REGULAR treats them as ordinary cells; INCLUDE_AND_LABEL includes them with a distinct multinucleated class label.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Allowed values are controlled by MULTINUCLEATED_CELL_POLICY_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "EXCLUDE".

parameters.name: EXCLUDE_CELLS_TOUCHING_IMAGE_EDGE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Configure exclude cells touching image edge for Vascular."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Excludes Cellpose-derived cells touching the image boundary before vascular quantification to avoid edge-truncated measurement artifacts.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: WALL_MARKER_OVERLAP_COMPARTMENT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cell compartment used to test overlap with the smooth-muscle wall."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cell compartment used to test overlap with the smooth-muscle wall. Cytoplasm avoids counting nucleus-only overlap as wall marker support.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Allowed values are controlled by WALL_MARKER_OVERLAP_COMPARTMENT_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "cytoplasm".

parameters.name: ENDOTHELIUM_MARKER_OVERLAP_COMPARTMENT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cell compartment used to test overlap with endothelium."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cell compartment used to test overlap with endothelium. Cell-level overlap is the default because endothelial contact can occur outside the nucleus.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Allowed values are controlled by ENDOTHELIUM_MARKER_OVERLAP_COMPARTMENT_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "cell".

parameters.name: VSMC_CLASSIFICATION_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how vascular smooth-muscle cells are classified."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: VSMC classification rule. The default requires a nucleus centroid outside the lumen, inside the filled SMA band, and sufficiently overlapping the filled SMA band.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Allowed values are controlled by VSMC_CLASSIFICATION_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "NUCLEUS_IN_FILLED_SMA_BAND".

parameters.name: MIN_VSMC_NUCLEUS_FILLED_SMA_OVERLAP_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Require this nuclear overlap with the filled SMA band."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum nucleus ROI overlap with the filled SMA band required for VSMC classification under the nucleus-gated anatomy mode.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: 0.95d.

parameters.name: USE_CD31_ENDOTHELIAL_EXCLUSION_FOR_VSMC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Optionally exclude CD31-overlapping nuclei from VSMC calls."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional advanced exclusion that blocks VSMC classification when the selected endothelial compartment meets the CD31 overlap threshold. Disabled by default because VSMC identity is nucleus-gated to the SMA band.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: MIN_SMOOTH_MUSCLE_OVERLAP_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum selected-compartment overlap fraction required to classify a cell as smooth-muscle associated."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum selected-compartment overlap fraction required to classify a cell as smooth-muscle associated.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: 0.50d.

parameters.name: MIN_ENDOTHELIUM_OVERLAP_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum selected-compartment overlap fraction required to classify a cell as endothelial associated."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:

parameters.name: ALLOW_DOUBLE_POSITIVE_CELLS_AS_VSMC

parameters.name: EXCLUDE_CELLS_WITH_NUCLEUS_OVERLAPPING_LUMEN

parameters.name: ENDOTHELIUM_BAND_RADIUS_MULTIPLIER

parameters.name: WALL_MIN_SUPPORTED_ANGLE_FRACTION

parameters.name: WALL_MIN_RADIAL_SUPPORT_DENSITY
