What it does: Absolute whole-cell best-parameters file used when CELL_PARAM_SOURCE is BEST_PARAMS_FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: USE_GPU
parameters.helpGroovy: "Run Cellpose on GPU when the configured runtime supports it."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Requests GPU acceleration for Cellpose execution. Disable only when the configured runtime/GPU is known to be unavailable; this affects runtime execution, not analysis rules.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: TILE_SIZE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Tile size in pixels used for Cellpose inference."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Tile size in pixels used for Cellpose inference. Change only to balance memory use and boundary behavior after testing on representative images.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1024.

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Batch compatible Cellpose work across images or regions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Enables ASTRA's batch-oriented Cellpose runtime behavior. Leave enabled for normal project runs unless diagnosing runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use image calibration for physical-scale Cellpose input."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Uses image calibration to convert micron diameter settings into pixel-space Cellpose inputs. Disable only for controlled diagnostic runs.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_GUI_NOTIFICATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Shows QuPath notifications for run completion and fatal stops."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Shows QuPath notifications for run completion and fatal stops. This affects user visibility only and does not change scientific output.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SHOW_VIEWER_PROGRESS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Attempts to move the QuPath viewer to the image/region currently being processed."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Attempts to move the QuPath viewer to the image/region currently being processed. This is display-only and does not affect measurements.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: FOCUS_VIEWER_ON_IMAGE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Opens or focuses the current project image during GUI-visible progress updates."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Opens or focuses the current project image during GUI-visible progress updates. This is display-only and safe to disable for batch runs.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: FOCUS_VIEWER_ON_ROI
parameters.helpGroovy: "Centers the viewer around the active analysis ROI during progress updates."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Centers the viewer around the active analysis ROI during progress updates. This is display-only and does not alter object hierarchy state.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: VIEWER_FOCUS_PADDING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Extra viewer padding around focused ROIs."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Extra viewer padding around focused ROIs. Change only for display ergonomics; it has no effect on segmentation or measurement.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: 1.35d.

parameters.name: VIEWER_FOCUS_MIN_VIEW_SIZE_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum viewer size in pixels when focusing small ROIs."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum viewer size in pixels when focusing small ROIs. This affects only how progress is displayed in QuPath.

When to change: Change this only while intentionally testing internals or reproducing a specific protocol.

Analysis consequence: This is an advanced override for developers or scripted validation.

Default value: 256.0d.

parameters.name: LOG_VIEWER_PROGRESS_FAILURES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Enable log viewer progress failures diagnostics."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Logs viewer-focus failures for diagnostics. Enable when troubleshooting GUI progress display; failures do not alter analysis results.

When to change: Change this when auditing a run or investigating a failure.

Analysis consequence: This changes audit verbosity or diagnostic artifacts without changing intended analysis logic.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which project images or analysis regions are analyzed. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image, SELECTED_ANALYSIS_REGION uses the currently selected annotation whose class matches CLASS_ANALYSIS_REGION, and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION, matches SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES against original imported image names instead of QuPath display names. Use only when project entries were renamed.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: DETECTION_TARGET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls which segmentation objects are produced before colocalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which segmentation objects are produced before colocalization. Use NUCLEUS for nucleus-only measurements, CELL for whole-cell measurements without nuclei, and BOTH when Cell/Cytoplasm and Nucleus compartments must be evaluated together.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by DETECTION_TARGET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "NUCLEUS".

parameters.name: SEGMENTATION_PRESET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the Cellpose-SAM inference preset."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: GUI-friendly Cellpose-SAM inference preset. BALANCED matches ASTRA's previous defaults, STRICT reduces weak detections, SENSITIVE recovers weaker objects, and CUSTOM uses the explicit advanced detector knobs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Allowed values are controlled by SEGMENTATION_PRESET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SEGMENTATION_PRESET_BALANCED.

Example: use BALANCED for routine runs, STRICT for fewer weak detections, SENSITIVE for weak-object recovery, and CUSTOM only after deliberate tuning.

parameters.name: NUCLEUS_SEGMENTATION_CHANNELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by nucleus segmentation channels."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channels used only for nucleus segmentation. Change this when the nuclear marker is not the first image channel or when the nucleus detector should use multiple channels; these channels do not define colocalization positivity by themselves.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: ["DAPI"].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: NUC_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by nucleus diameter um."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus diameter in microns for Cellpose. The default 0.0 leaves diameter unspecified, matching upstream Cellpose auto-diameter behavior; set a positive value only after validating that nucleus masks are consistently over- or under-split.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_CELLPROB
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose cellprob acceptance for nucleus cellprob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus cellprob threshold. The default 0.0 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; increasing it makes detection stricter and can remove weak nucleus masks before measurement.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_FLOW
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose flow acceptance for nucleus flow."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus flow threshold. The default 0.4 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; changing it alters which nucleus masks are accepted.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_NITER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose iteration count for nucleus niter."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus iteration count. The default 0 omits the explicit niter parameter so Cellpose uses its automatic/proportional runtime behavior; positive values force a fixed iteration count.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted nucleus mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest nucleus mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny specks or fragmented nuclear debris. Decrease it only if true nuclei are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream classification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: CELL_SEGMENTATION_CHANNELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by cell segmentation channels."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Image channels used only for whole-cell segmentation. Change this to the membrane/cytoplasm channels that define cell boundaries; do not include nuclear channels unless they are intentionally part of the cell-boundary model.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: [].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: CELL_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by cell diameter um."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Whole-cell diameter in microns for Cellpose. The default 0.0 leaves diameter unspecified, matching upstream Cellpose auto-diameter behavior; set a positive value only after validating that cell masks are consistently too small or too large.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO.

parameters.name: CELL_CELLPROB
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose cellprob acceptance for cell cellprob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell cellprob threshold. The default 0.0 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; increasing it makes detection stricter and can remove weak cell masks before cell-level measurement.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: CELL_FLOW
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose flow acceptance for cell flow."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell flow threshold. The default 0.4 matches upstream Cellpose inference behavior; changing it alters which cell masks are accepted.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: CELL_NITER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose iteration count for cell niter."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose whole-cell iteration count. The default 0 omits the explicit niter parameter so Cellpose uses its automatic/proportional runtime behavior; positive values force a fixed iteration count.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO.

parameters.name: CELL_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted cell mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest whole-cell mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny debris or fragmented cell masks. Decrease it only if true cells are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream quantification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection. The default 0.0 disables simplification; increasing it can change mask geometry and downstream measurements.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMIN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. The ASTRA default is 1.0; change only after image-intensity review because it affects detector input scaling and segmentation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMAX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. The ASTRA default is 99.0; change only after image-intensity review because it affects detector input scaling and segmentation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMAX.

parameters.name: SAM_NTHREADS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose-SAM thread count."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose-SAM thread count. Zero leaves runtime threading automatic; changing it affects performance, not marker positivity logic.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: 0.

parameters.name: SAM_READ_RESULTS_ASYNC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running. Leave disabled unless runtime profiling shows a benefit; it affects execution behavior, not scoring rules.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: EXCLUDE_CELLS_TOUCHING_IMAGE_EDGE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Removes detected cells touching the image boundary before quantification."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Removes detected cells touching the image boundary before quantification. Enable when edge-truncated cells would bias colocalization measurements.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: INTENSITY_DOWNSAMPLE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Downsample used for intensity measurement."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Downsample used for intensity measurement. Increase only for speed after validating measurement stability because it affects threshold inputs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1.0d.

parameters.name: COLOCALIZATION_CHECKS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Define marker, channel, and compartment checks."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Defines the marker combinations to score. Each check has an output label, one measurement compartment, channels that must all be positive, and optional excluded channels that use object/compartment presence instead of numeric thresholding.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: ASSIGN_COLOCALIZATION_CLASSES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for assign colocalization classes."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When true, quantified cells receive QuPath object classes from the configured colocalization calls for visual inspection. Measurements and exports are still written either way.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: COLOCALIZATION_CLASS_PREFIX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for colocalization class prefix."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Text prepended to positive colocalization classes. Keep a clear ASTRA prefix so generated visual classes are distinguishable from manual classes.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "ASTRA ".

parameters.name: COLOCALIZATION_NEGATIVE_CLASS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for colocalization negative class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Class assigned when the configured visual colocalization check is negative.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: "ASTRA Negative".

parameters.name: CLASSIFY_BY_FIRST_CHECK_ONLY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for classify by first check only."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When multiple checks are configured, classify cells by the first check only. Disable only when you want deterministic composite classes from all configured checks.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: INCLUDE_MARKER_PATTERN_IN_CLASS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for include marker pattern in class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When true, positive class names include the check label so visual classes identify the marker pattern used.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for expression classification mode."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Default PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE learns expression boundaries from analysis-region pixels, scores within-cell pixel levels, and classifies cells from ordinal-score KDE valleys. LEGACY_BINARY keeps the historical cell-mean positive/negative path for compatibility.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Allowed values are controlled by EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE".

parameters.name: DISPLAY_COLOCALIZATION_CHECK
parameters.helpGroovy: "Colocalization check label used for the QuPath visual PathClass in PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE mode."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Colocalization check label used for the QuPath visual PathClass in PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE mode. Blank means the first configured check.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: POSITIVITY_METHOD
parameters.helpGroovy: "Legacy binary method used only when EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE is LEGACY_BINARY."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary method used only when EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE is LEGACY_BINARY. MEAN_INTENSITY compares compartment mean intensity; PIXEL_POSITIVE_FRACTION compares the fraction of compartment pixels above threshold.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Allowed values are controlled by POSITIVITY_METHOD_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "MEAN_INTENSITY".

parameters.name: PIXEL_POSITIVE_FRACTION_MIN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Legacy binary cutoff used only when EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE is LEGACY_BINARY and POSITIVITY_METHOD is PIXEL_POSITIVE_FRACTION."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary cutoff used only when EXPRESSION_CLASSIFICATION_MODE is LEGACY_BINARY and POSITIVITY_METHOD is PIXEL_POSITIVE_FRACTION.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.05d.

parameters.name: THRESHOLD_POPULATION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for threshold population."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary threshold population. PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE always uses PIXEL_INTENSITY from analysis-region pixels regardless of this field.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Allowed values are controlled by THRESHOLD_POPULATION_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "PIXEL_INTENSITY".

parameters.name: THRESHOLD_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for threshold mode."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: THRESHOLD_MODE chooses the thresholding method for every non-excluded marker. Per-marker threshold methods are intentionally unsupported.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Allowed values are controlled by THRESHOLD_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "LOG_KDE_VALLEY".

parameters.name: GMM_COMPONENTS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Number of Gaussian components used by GAUSSIAN_MIXTURE and LOG_GAUSSIAN_MIXTURE boundary inference."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Number of Gaussian components used by GAUSSIAN_MIXTURE and LOG_GAUSSIAN_MIXTURE boundary inference.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 2.

parameters.name: OTSU_CLASS_COUNT
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the QuPath class or classifier name for otsu class count."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Number of ordinal classes used by AUTO_OTSU_PER_CHANNEL boundary inference.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 2.

parameters.name: THRESHOLD_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for threshold scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: THRESHOLD_SCOPE chooses the population scope used to resolve thresholds: IMAGE resolves per image, REGION resolves per parent analysis region, and SELECTED_IMAGES uses the explicit threshold-source image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by THRESHOLD_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "IMAGE".

parameters.name: THRESHOLD_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for threshold selected image names."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used only when THRESHOLD_SCOPE is SELECTED_IMAGES. These images provide the threshold-source population and do not automatically run Cellpose unless they are also included by IMAGE_SCOPE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_THRESHOLD_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for match threshold image names against original."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When THRESHOLD_SCOPE is SELECTED_IMAGES, matches THRESHOLD_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES against original imported image names instead of QuPath display names. Use only when project entries were renamed.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: MANUAL_INTENSITY_BOUNDARIES_BY_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Manual ordered expression boundaries used when THRESHOLD_MODE is MANUAL."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Manual ordered expression boundaries used when THRESHOLD_MODE is MANUAL. Keys match Channel|Compartment markers and values may be a number or ordered list of numbers.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: [:].

parameters.name: MANUAL_INTENSITY_THRESHOLDS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for manual intensity thresholds."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary manual positivity thresholds used when THRESHOLD_MODE is MANUAL and no ordered manual boundary list is supplied.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: [:].

parameters.name: RANGE_THRESHOLD_FRACTIONS_BY_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for range threshold fractions by marker."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Ordered range-percent expression boundary fractions used when THRESHOLD_MODE is RANGE_PERCENT. Values may be a number or list of fractions between 0 and 1.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: [:].

parameters.name: RANGE_THRESHOLD_FRACTION_BY_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for range threshold fraction by marker."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary range-percent threshold fractions used when THRESHOLD_MODE is RANGE_PERCENT and no ordered fraction list is supplied.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: [:].

parameters.name: THRESHOLD_PROVENANCE_BY_MARKER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for threshold provenance by marker."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Audit text required for manual thresholds. Describe the real threshold source before publication use; placeholders are rejected in MANUAL mode.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: [:].

parameters.name: BACKGROUND_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for background mode."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Legacy binary background subtraction mode. PIXEL_LEVEL_SCORE requires NONE because regional pixels define the modeled signal/background space directly. In LEGACY_BINARY, LOCAL_PERCENTILE computes a data-derived intensity offset from the active threshold population.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Allowed values are controlled by BACKGROUND_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "NONE".

parameters.name: LOCAL_BACKGROUND_PERCENTILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for local background percentile."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Percentile fraction used by LOCAL_PERCENTILE background correction. Example: 0.05 subtracts the fifth percentile of raw marker intensities from each marker in the active threshold population.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.05d.

parameters.name: BACKGROUND_SUBTRACTION_BY_CHANNEL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by background subtraction by channel."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Manual per-marker intensity offsets used only when BACKGROUND_MODE is MANUAL_OFFSET. Each Channel|Compartment value is subtracted from that marker's measured mean before thresholding and positivity calls; keys must match COLOCALIZATION_CHECKS.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: [:].

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: EXPORT_QC_FIGURES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Control whether ASTRA writes export qc figures."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exports threshold QC figures for non-excluded markers. Disable only when plots are intentionally unnecessary; requested export failures are not hidden.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: RESULTS_FOLDER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the project-relative ASTRA results folder."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project-relative analysis results folder under projectRoot/astra/results. Colocalization defaults to astra/results/colocalization.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "colocalization".

parameters.name: RESULTS_BASENAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the exported result filename prefix."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional filename prefix for exported results. Leave blank to derive names from the slide/project entry; set for publication-specific naming.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: SMA_MEDIA_CHANNEL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by sma media channel."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact image channel containing the SMA/wall signal used when optional radial media inference is enabled. Manual wall annotations remain controlled by CLASS_SMA_MEDIA_REGION.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "SMA".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: NUCLEAR_CHANNEL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by nuclear channel."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact image channel used for nucleus Cellpose detection and nuclear-signal QC measurements.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "DAPI".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: MARKER_SIGNAL_CHANNEL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the image channel names used by marker signal channel."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact image channel containing the nuclear marker signal to score inside SMA-gated nuclei.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which fluorescence signal is interpreted as each biological channel or marker.

Default value: "Marker".

Example: rename this when the image channel label differs from the ASTRA default, not when the biology is unchanged.

parameters.name: MARKER_SIGNAL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Publication-facing marker name used in run summaries and classifier descriptions."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Publication-facing marker name used in run summaries and classifier descriptions. This can differ from the raw image channel name.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: "Marker".

parameters.name: USE_MANUAL_SMA_MEDIA_ANNOTATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Publication rescue default."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Publication rescue default. Use manual CLASS_SMA_MEDIA_REGION annotations that overlap the analysis ROI as the wall/media compartment.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: MEDIA_OVERLAP_FRACTION_MIN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels that must overlap the active Smooth Muscle/media annotation before Marker scoring."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels that must overlap the active Smooth Muscle/media annotation before Marker scoring. The publication rescue default is 0.90 to exclude likely endothelial/lumen-border nuclei while tolerating small edge spillover.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: 0.90d.

parameters.name: MARKER_BACKGROUND_MIN_PIXELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for marker background min pixels."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum non-nuclear Marker background pixel count required for the local perinuclear background; when local support is lower, the tool uses non-nuclear pixels in the same Smooth Muscle/media annotation. If both are lower, the nucleus is rejected from Marker scoring.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 100.

parameters.name: MARKER_BACKGROUND_INNER_DIAMETER_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by marker background inner diameter fraction."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Inner buffer radius for the perinuclear Marker background annulus, expressed as a fraction of the median detected nucleus diameter in the region.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 0.20d.

parameters.name: MARKER_BACKGROUND_OUTER_DIAMETER_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by marker background outer diameter fraction."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Outer buffer radius for the perinuclear Marker background annulus, expressed as a fraction of the median detected nucleus diameter in the region.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 0.90d.

parameters.name: MARKER_BACKGROUND_MIN_WIDTH_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for marker background min width px."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum full-resolution pixel width of the perinuclear Marker background annulus.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 2.0d.

parameters.name: MARKER_DIFFUSE_BACKGROUND_QUANTILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for marker diffuse background quantile."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Background quantile used by the diffuse nuclear Marker arm. Nuclear pixels above this local/background P95 threshold are evaluated by a one-sided binomial tail test.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.95d.

parameters.name: MARKER_DIFFUSE_NULL_TAIL_PROBABILITY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Null exceedance probability for the diffuse Marker arm, matching the P95 background threshold."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Null exceedance probability for the diffuse Marker arm, matching the P95 background threshold.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.05d.

parameters.name: MARKER_DIFFUSE_MIN_ENRICHED_PIXELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum number of nuclear pixels above the diffuse Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum number of nuclear pixels above the diffuse Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 5.

parameters.name: MARKER_DIFFUSE_MIN_ENRICHED_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels above the diffuse Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels above the diffuse Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.10d.

parameters.name: MARKER_FOCAL_BACKGROUND_QUANTILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set local background behavior for marker focal background quantile."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Background quantile used by the focal nuclear Marker arm. This stricter P99 threshold captures small bright nuclear puncta without accepting single-pixel artifacts.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.99d.

parameters.name: MARKER_FOCAL_NULL_TAIL_PROBABILITY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Null exceedance probability for the focal Marker arm, matching the P99 background threshold."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Null exceedance probability for the focal Marker arm, matching the P99 background threshold.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.01d.

parameters.name: MARKER_FOCAL_MIN_ENRICHED_PIXELS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum number of nuclear pixels above the focal Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum number of nuclear pixels above the focal Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 3.

parameters.name: MARKER_FOCAL_MIN_ENRICHED_FRACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels above the focal Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Minimum fraction of nuclear pixels above the focal Marker threshold required after BH-FDR significance is met.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.02d.

parameters.name: MARKER_FDR_Q_THRESHOLD
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the threshold behavior for marker fdr q threshold."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted q-value cutoff used independently by the diffuse and focal Marker arms within each analysis ROI.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes intensity calls, local background handling, or marker positivity.

Default value: 0.05d.

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which project image entries are processed by the marker-rescue tool. Current Image processes the open viewer image, Selected Region processes only the selected ROI annotation in the open viewer image, and Project Image Selection processes the named project entries chosen in the launcher.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact project image names to process when Image Scope is Project Image Selection. Use the launcher image selector to add one, many, or all project images.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: MATCH_SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES_AGAINST_ORIGINAL
parameters.helpGroovy: "Match selected images by original import name instead of display name."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: When enabled, selected project image names are matched against the original imported image name rather than the current QuPath project display name.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: ONESHOT_RUN_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Configure oneshot run mode for Sma Af647 Oneshot."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls whether marker rescue runs Cellpose detection plus quantification, Cellpose detection only, quantification/export only on existing marker-rescue cells, or project-state export from saved marker-rescue cells without mutation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Allowed values are controlled by ONESHOT_RUN_MODE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "FULL".

parameters.name: SCRIPT_ACTION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Internal launcher action. RUN executes the selected marker-rescue mode; EXPORT rebuilds CSVs from saved project marker-rescue cells without Cellpose, quantification, cleanup, or hierarchy mutation."

parameters.name: CLASS_ANALYSIS_REGION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the full analysis ROI class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath annotation class used as the full analysis ROI where Cellpose runs. SMA wall/media inclusion is handled separately by CLASS_SMA_MEDIA_REGION or SMA-derived media inference.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "ROI".

parameters.name: CLASS_SMA_MEDIA_REGION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the SMA wall/media annotation class."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath annotation class for manual SMA wall/media annotations that overlap each analysis ROI. Used when USE_MANUAL_SMA_MEDIA_ANNOTATIONS is true; the media mask is clipped to the analysis ROI for post-Cellpose inclusion/exclusion without clipping Cellpose input.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes how detected objects are assigned to biological classes.

Default value: "Smooth Muscle".

parameters.name: CLEAR_EXISTING_ONESHOT_OBJECTS_FIRST
parameters.helpGroovy: "Internal cleanup flag. Cellpose reruns replace previous marker-rescue objects for each successfully refreshed ROI so repeated runs do not duplicate cells."

parameters.name: ADD_MISSING_ONESHOT_OBJECTS_ONLY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Addition mode for Cellpose runs."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Addition mode for Cellpose runs. When enabled, marker rescue processes only analysis ROIs in the selected image scope that contain no existing detection/cell objects; ROIs that already contain detections are left untouched.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: SEGMENTATION_PRESET
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the Cellpose-SAM inference preset."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: GUI-friendly Cellpose-SAM inference preset for marker rescue nuclei. BALANCED matches ASTRA's previous defaults, STRICT reduces weak detections, SENSITIVE recovers weaker objects, and CUSTOM uses the explicit advanced detector knobs.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Allowed values are controlled by SEGMENTATION_PRESET_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SEGMENTATION_PRESET_BALANCED.

Example: use BALANCED for routine runs, STRICT for fewer weak detections, SENSITIVE for weak-object recovery, and CUSTOM only after deliberate tuning.

parameters.name: WRITE_RECONSTRUCTED_LUMEN_ANNOTATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Control whether ASTRA writes write reconstructed lumen annotations."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: QC display. Measurements are always written; when enabled, ASTRA also creates visible reconstructed lumen annotations under each analysis ROI.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: USE_MANUAL_LUMEN_DIAMETER_ANNOTATIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by use manual lumen diameter annotations."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Include manually drawn lumen-diameter line annotations in marker-rescue exports. Enable when each analysis ROI may contain a user-drawn lumen diameter annotation.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: CLASS_LUMEN_DIAMETER_ANNOTATION
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by class lumen diameter annotation."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact QuPath class name for optional manually drawn lumen-diameter line annotations. Used only when USE_MANUAL_LUMEN_DIAMETER_ANNOTATIONS is enabled.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: "Diameter".

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose how ASTRA resolves nucleus model."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model source. MODEL_NAME uses a named Cellpose model such as cpsam; FILE uses one explicit model file path.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Allowed values are controlled by NUC_MODEL_SOURCE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.MODEL_SOURCE_MODEL_NAME.

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_NAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the Cellpose model name for nucleus."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus Cellpose model name used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is MODEL_NAME.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.MODEL_NAME_CPSAM.

parameters.name: NUC_MODEL_FILE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Use this explicit model file when file source is selected."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Absolute nucleus model file path used when NUC_MODEL_SOURCE is FILE.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the Cellpose model or saved parameter source used for segmentation.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the physical diameter used by nucleus diameter um."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Nucleus diameter in microns for Cellpose. The default 0.0 leaves diameter unspecified so Cellpose uses its own runtime behavior.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.DIAMETER_UM_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_CELLPROB
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose cellprob acceptance for nucleus cellprob."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus cellprob threshold. Increasing it makes nucleus detection stricter.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.CELLPROB_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_FLOW
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose flow acceptance for nucleus flow."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus flow threshold. Changing it alters which nucleus masks are accepted.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.FLOW_THRESHOLD.

parameters.name: NUC_NITER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set Cellpose iteration count for nucleus niter."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose nucleus iteration count. The default 0 omits the explicit niter parameter.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.NITER_AUTO.

parameters.name: NUC_MIN_MASK_EQUIVALENT_DIAMETER_UM
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the minimum accepted nucleus mask diameter in microns."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Sets the smallest nucleus mask ASTRA keeps after Cellpose segmentation, expressed as equivalent circular diameter in microns. This is the user-facing physical-size guard behind the Cellpose minimum-size behavior.

When to change: Increase it to remove tiny specks or fragmented nuclear debris. Decrease it only if true nuclei are visibly smaller in this assay.

Analysis consequence: Raising this value reduces weak or tiny detections before downstream classification.

Default value: 1.0 um.

parameters.name: TILE_SIZE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose tile size in pixels used during nucleus detection."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose tile size in pixels used during nucleus detection.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1024.

parameters.name: USE_GPU
parameters.helpGroovy: "Run Cellpose on GPU when the configured runtime supports it."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Runs Cellpose with GPU acceleration when the configured ASTRA runtime supports it. Disable only when CPU execution is intentional.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional contour simplification distance in pixels after Cellpose-SAM detection. The default 0.0 disables simplification.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_SIMPLIFY_DISTANCE_PX.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMIN
parameters.helpGroovy: "Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Lower percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. This affects detector input scaling, not raw SMA/Marker measurement thresholds.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMIN.

parameters.name: SAM_NORM_PMAX
parameters.helpGroovy: "Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Upper percentile for Cellpose-SAM image normalization. This affects detector input scaling, not raw SMA/Marker measurement thresholds.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: CellposeInferenceDefaults.SAM_NORM_PMAX.

parameters.name: SAM_NTHREADS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Cellpose-SAM thread count."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Cellpose-SAM thread count. Zero leaves runtime threading automatic.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: 0.

parameters.name: SAM_READ_RESULTS_ASYNC
parameters.helpGroovy: "Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Reads Cellpose mask outputs while the process is still running. Leave disabled unless runtime profiling shows a benefit.

When to change: Change this when moving between workstations or diagnosing runtime behavior.

Analysis consequence: This changes execution speed or hardware use. It should not change intended biology when calibration stays enabled.

Default value: false.

parameters.name: INTENSITY_DOWNSAMPLE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Downsample used for nucleus/media overlap and Marker local-background measurements."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Downsample used for nucleus/media overlap and Marker local-background measurements. Keep 1.0 for publication rescue unless speed is more important than exact pixel sampling.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes Cellpose object recovery and can alter downstream cell counts and measurements.

Default value: 1.0d.

parameters.name: EXPORT_TECHNICAL_CSV
parameters.helpGroovy: "Write detailed technical QC rows for audit."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Writes the publication rescue technical CSV with Smooth Muscle overlap, Marker background source, diffuse/focal thresholds, p-values, q-values, call reason, and final binary call.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: true.

parameters.name: RESULTS_FOLDER
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose the project-relative ASTRA results folder."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project-relative results folder under projectRoot/astra/results for the marker-rescue CSV files.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "sma_marker_rescue".

parameters.name: RESULTS_BASENAME
parameters.helpGroovy: "Set the exported result filename prefix."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Optional filename prefix for exported marker-rescue results. Leave blank to use sma_marker_rescue.

When to change: Change this when you need different exported artifacts or result locations.

Analysis consequence: This changes files or visible QC annotations written by ASTRA.

Default value: "".

parameters.name: NUC_PARAM_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Hidden marker-rescue setting: nucleus Cellpose parameters are read directly from this dialog."

parameters.name: NUC_SAVED_MODEL_ID
parameters.helpGroovy: "Hidden marker-rescue setting used by the shared Cellpose model-resolution path."

parameters.name: USE_BATCH_MODE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Hidden marker-rescue setting: batches Cellpose ROI tiles across selected project images."

parameters.name: USE_PIXEL_SCALING
parameters.helpGroovy: "Hidden marker-rescue setting: keeps Cellpose pixel scaling enabled for batched inference."

parameters.name: REGION_SOURCE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Chooses how output regions are created: copy existing source-class ROIs or generate deterministic random rectangles."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Chooses how output regions are created: copy existing source-class ROIs or generate deterministic random rectangles.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: "FROM_ROI".

parameters.name: DISTRIBUTION_STRATEGY
parameters.helpGroovy: "Controls how the requested total region count is distributed across eligible images."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls how the requested total region count is distributed across eligible images.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which workflow branch executes. Review the selected mode before publication runs.

Default value: "UNIFORM_PER_IMAGE".

parameters.name: IMAGE_SCOPE
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose which project image entries this run should process."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Controls which project images receive generated regions. CURRENT_IMAGE uses the open image; PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION uses the explicit selected-image list.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Allowed values are controlled by IMAGE_SCOPE_OPTIONS in the manifest/script contract.

Default value: "CURRENT_IMAGE".

Example: use CURRENT_IMAGE for a live check and PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION for audited project batches.

parameters.name: SELECTED_IMAGE_NAMES
parameters.helpGroovy: "Choose project images for selected-image scope."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Project image names used when IMAGE_SCOPE is PROJECT_IMAGE_SELECTION. The launcher writes this audited list in project display-name order.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes the images included in the run, but not the scientific thresholds themselves.

Default value: [].

parameters.name: TOTAL_REGIONS
parameters.helpGroovy: "Exact number of output region annotations to create."
parameters.detailsMarkdown:
What it does: Exact number of output region annotations to create. The script fails rather than saving a partial set.

When to change: Change this only when the project, staining panel, model source, or analysis protocol requires it.

Analysis consequence: This changes which QuPath annotations or classifiers ASTRA reads or writes.

Default value: 24.
